Common_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for improving anthocyanin sequestration and stable pigment accumulation in biofortified tomato fruit.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely assists anthocyanin transport into the vacuole and thereby supports anthocyanin accumulation in Del/Ros1 tomato fruit.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as induced transcript by suppression subtractive hybridization and expression profiling; functional role inferred from annotation and pathway schematic.
Common_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Suppression subtractive hybridization of Del/Ros1N versus wild-type fruit followed by expression validation.
Common_Genes.Genetic_Population: Del/Ros1N transgenic versus wild type; supported across independent lines.
Common_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Associated fruit anthocyanins quantified by spectrophotometry and HPLC.
Common_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: In pathway schematic, GST is proposed to participate in anthocyanin transport into the vacuole rather than catalyze anthocyanin biosynthesis; no direct transport assay reported.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization; PROFOOD microarray ArrayExpress E-MEXP-1830, platform A-MEXP-1417.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Manipulating PAL offers leverage over pathway flux for anthocyanin biofortification in tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to produce cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: None reported here; ammonia lyase reaction does not require redox cofactor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Del/Ros1 strongly induce PAL transcript and activity, increasing flux through phenylpropanoid metabolism and contributing to very high anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot for transcript induction and direct PAL enzyme activity assay in ripe fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening tomato fruit in Del/Ros1 transgenic lines, especially ripe fruit of line N.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established enzyme assignment and direct PAL activity measurement in fruit extracts.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: PAL transcript/activity induction precedes enhanced phenylpropanoid flux; authors state PAL activity determines flux through phenylpropanoid metabolism in Solanaceous species.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known anthocyanin pathway gene identified as differentially expressed by suppression subtractive hybridization and validated by northern blot.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Wild type versus Del/Ros1 transgenic lines C and N in MicroTom.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening and Del/Ros1 transgene expression.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Only PAL showed detectable expression in wild-type fruit among assayed anthocyanin genes; Del/Ros1 increased activity about 200-fold in line N.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization; northern blot; PROFOOD microarray ArrayExpress E-MEXP-1830.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Associated with increased peel flavonols as well as anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry step supplying flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad flux increase may alter multiple phenylpropanoids, not only anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: PAL activity increased ~200-fold in Del/Ros1 line N.
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit peel and flesh / ripe fruit extracts

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful target for increasing flavonoid flux toward anthocyanins in engineered fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones to dihydroflavonols, converting naringenin to dihydrokaempferol.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: 2-oxoglutarate, Fe2+, O2, ascorbate typically required for 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenases; not explicitly measured here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its induction supports enhanced conversion of flavanones into anthocyanin precursors in Del/Ros1 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot validation of transcript induction.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known flavonoid pathway enzyme assignment from gene identity; expression induced in transgenic fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: F3H is the canonical step from flavanone to dihydroflavonol precursor upstream of anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Examined as candidate pathway gene and verified by northern blot because it was not recovered in subtraction library.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Wild type and Del/Ros1 transgenic fruit (line N and independent lines).
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Associated metabolite changes measured by HPLC and anthocyanin assay.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Specifically noted as not identified by suppression subtractive hybridization but induced according to northern blot.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Northern blot; PROFOOD microarray support for pathway induction.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Transcript induced in Del/Ros1 fruit; no exact fold given.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A promising target because CHI activity may limit flavonoid flux in tomato skin; induction may support high anthocyanin/flavonol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chalcones to flavanones, converting naringenin chalcone to naringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: No cofactor required for canonical CHI activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Del/Ros1 increase CHI activity transiently during fruit development, potentially relieving a flux limitation in tomato flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chalcone isomerase enzyme activity assay during fruit development; pathway/metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Direct enzyme activity increase measured in transgenic fruit and prior known gene function.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Authors note CHI may limit flux through flavonoid metabolism in tomato skin; its induction coincides with enhanced anthocyanin/flavonol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known candidate pathway enzyme analyzed by activity assay and pathway context.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Wild type and Del/Ros1 transgenic lines.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Authors specifically contrast Del/Ros1 with Lc/C1, noting Del/Ros1 induced CHI activity whereas Lc/C1 did not.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Also likely contributes to elevated peel flavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: CHI activity increased threefold, transiently during fruit development.
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit tissues, especially skin context discussed

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Critical target for producing delphinidin/petunidin-type anthocyanins in tomato and overcoming substrate constraints in DFR.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates dihydroflavonols at the 3' and 5' positions, converting dihydrokaempferol toward dihydromyricetin-type precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Likely requires 2-oxoglutarate, Fe2+, O2, ascorbate as a dioxygenase; not directly assayed here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of F3'5'H by Del/Ros1 enables formation of the proper DFR substrate and is presented as a key reason anthocyanins accumulate in tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression induction reported from northern/microarray pathway analysis; supported by anthocyanin composition dominated by delphinidin/petunidin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Anthocyanin products identified are delphinidin/petunidin derivatives consistent with F3'5'H activity; gene activation discussed as mechanistically crucial.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Authors explain tomato DFR is specific for dihydromyricetin-type substrate, so F3'5'H is required upstream to supply appropriate precursor for anthocyanin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known pathway gene identified among induced anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in transgenic fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Wild type versus Del/Ros1 transgenic lines.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC-PDA and ESI-Q-TOF MS/MS for anthocyanin identities.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Authors contrast with maize Lc/C1 system, which did not activate F3'5'H and failed to induce anthocyanins in tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Subtractive hybridization/microarray pathway induction; metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Determines anthocyanin type toward delphinidin/petunidin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can redirect dihydroflavonol branch composition away from other flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Qualitatively associated with accumulation of delphinidin and petunidin acylated glycosides; no fold reported.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important enzyme target because tomato DFR substrate specificity constrains anthocyanin engineering outcomes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the anthocyanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: NADPH typically required; not directly assayed here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in the induced anthocyanin pathway; substrate specificity for dihydromyricetin-type intermediates is highlighted as a determinant of successful anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression induction as part of induced anthocyanin pathway; pathway logic supported by anthocyanin composition and prior substrate-specificity knowledge discussed by authors.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established anthocyanin pathway enzyme; authors specifically discuss substrate specificity in Solanaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts downstream of F3'5'H and upstream of ANS; requirement inferred from need to convert dihydromyricetin-type intermediates to anthocyanidins.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known anthocyanin biosynthetic gene included among induced pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Anthocyanin HPLC/MS profiling in associated fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes that tomato DFR will not accept dihydrokaempferol, explaining why activation of F3'5'H is necessary.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Core terminal anthocyanidin-forming step useful in engineering anthocyanin-rich fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: 2-oxoglutarate, Fe2+, O2, ascorbate typically required; not measured here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the induced anthocyanin gene set supporting accumulation of anthocyanidins that are further glycosylated and acylated in Del/Ros1 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression induction detected by subtractive hybridization/northern/microarray plus metabolite accumulation in transgenic fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established pathway assignment from gene identity and anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts immediately before glycosylation of anthocyanidins in the anthocyanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known pathway gene identified among induced anthocyanin biosynthesis transcripts.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: No gene-specific quantitative expression value reported.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization; microarray; northern blot for pathway genes.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Supports stable anthocyanin accumulation by forming glycosylated anthocyanins in biofortified fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to anthocyanidins at the 3-O position to generate anthocyanin 3-glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced expression likely enables stabilization of anthocyanidins into glycosylated anthocyanins that accumulate to high levels in fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression induction as part of Del/Ros1-responsive anthocyanin biosynthetic gene set; metabolite structures identified as glycosylated anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Anthocyanin products identified were glucosylated derivatives, consistent with 3-GT function.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts immediately after anthocyanidin synthesis to stabilize products for further modification.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known anthocyanin biosynthetic gene included in induced gene set.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC-PDA and ESI-MS/MS of anthocyanin glycosides.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for engineering final anthocyanin glycoside structures and potentially stability/quality traits.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to anthocyanin 3-O-glucosides, producing rutinoside-type anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: UDP-rhamnose or activated rhamnose donor inferred; not specified in paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to formation of rutinoside-containing anthocyanins that accumulate in purple tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression induction in transgenic fruit and structural identification of rutinoside-containing anthocyanins by HPLC-MS/MS.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Anthocyanins identified were rutinoside derivatives, consistent with RT activity.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Functions downstream of 3-GT in anthocyanin glycoside elaboration.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known anthocyanin modification gene identified in induced gene set.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Main anthocyanins in transgenic fruit were 3-rutinoside-5-glucoside derivatives acylated with cinnamic acids.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profiling and metabolite structural analysis.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for tailoring anthocyanin stability and final acylated forms in engineered fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl acyl groups to anthocyanin glycosides, generating acylated anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Likely acyl-CoA donor such as caffeoyl/coumaroyl/feruloyl-CoA; not directly tested.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induction likely enables accumulation of acylated anthocyanins, the major anthocyanin forms observed in purple tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression induction as putative anthocyanin acyltransferase plus HPLC-MS/MS identification of acylated anthocyanin products.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Product profile dominated by anthocyanins acylated with cinnamic acids; transcript identified as induced putative acyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts after glycosylation to decorate anthocyanins with aromatic acyl groups.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Suppression subtractive hybridization identified a putative anthocyanin acyltransferase transcript induced by Del/Ros1.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Del/Ros1N versus wild type, with support across independent transgenic lines.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC-PDA and ESI-MS/MS with alkaline/acid hydrolysis confirmation of acyl and sugar moieties.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Gene identity remains putative; biochemical assay of the enzyme itself was not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Affects anthocyanin chemical form and likely stability.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter pigment stability and bioavailability in complex ways.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Major products were acylated delphinidin and petunidin 3-rutinoside-5-glucosides; no expression fold specified.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful transgene for engineering high-anthocyanin tomato fruit with enhanced hydrophilic antioxidant capacity and potential dietary health benefits; supports biofortification through pathway-wide activation.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Fruit-specific due to tomato E8 promoter; visible pigmentation initiated at end of mature green stage and intensified during ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In combination with Ros1, induces strong anthocyanin accumulation throughout peel and flesh of tomato fruit and increases hydrophilic antioxidant capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic fruit-specific expression, northern blot validation of transgene and downstream gene induction, metabolite profiling by HPLC, PAL/CHI activity assays, antioxidant TEAC assay.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential component of the successful Del/Ros1 regulatory combination; broad activation of anthocyanin pathway genes enabled very high anthocyanin accumulation compared with prior TF combinations.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Tomato fruit ripening, especially late mature green through breaker/red stages.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct_or_unresolved; inferred direct transcriptional activator of anthocyanin pathway genes as a bHLH TF, but direct binding assays were not reported in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Transgenic E8::Del construct in combination with E8::Ros1
Regulation_Genes.Field_Trials: No field trials; greenhouse/transgenic line evaluation and mouse feeding study with freeze-dried fruit powder.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate transcription factor from Antirrhinum majus selected based on known role in anthocyanin biosynthesis; cDNA cloned into binary vector under E8 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Four independent transgenic tomato lines (Del/Ros1 C, N, Y, Z) in cv. MicroTom; phenotype also transferred to Money Maker, Ailsa Craig, and VF36 backgrounds by crossing.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Acts cooperatively with Ros1; authors state both MYB and bHLH factors together give much higher induction than either alone.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening-associated E8 promoter activity; fruit-specific expression.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis, side-chain modification, and vacuolar transport module activation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Spectrophotometric anthocyanin quantification, HPLC-PDA, ESI-Q-TOF MS/MS, TEAC antioxidant assay.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Del is a snapdragon basic helix-loop-helix TF. No abnormal anthocyanin accumulation was seen in vegetative tissues, consistent with fruit-specific promoter use.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization; PROFOOD tomato oligonucleotide microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-MEXP-1830; array platform A-MEXP-1417.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Intense purple fruit coloration in peel and flesh; no obvious vegetative abnormalities.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty reported; off-target induction of flavonols in peel also observed.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL, F3H, F3'5'H, DFR, ANS, glycosylation/modification genes including 3-GT, RT, putative anthocyanin acyltransferase, and transport-associated genes including GST and putative anthocyanin transporter.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In line N together with Ros1, fruit anthocyanin reached 2.83 ± 0.46 mg/g fresh weight; hydrophilic antioxidant capacity increased about threefold; PAL activity increased about 200-fold in line N.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates multiple anthocyanin biosynthetic and modification/transport genes in fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: total anthocyanin accumulation and hydrophilic antioxidant capacity
Regulation_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit peel and flesh of ripening/ripe tomato
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit ripening program via E8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: MicroTom (also transferred to Money Maker, Ailsa Craig, VF36)

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful transgene for anthocyanin biofortification of tomato fruit and for engineering health-promoting antioxidant-rich produce.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Fruit-specific due to E8 promoter; anthocyanin induction begins late mature green and intensifies during ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In combination with Del, activates anthocyanin biosynthesis leading to high anthocyanin accumulation in peel and flesh and elevated antioxidant capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fruit transgenesis, northern blot validation, suppression subtractive hybridization and microarray of downstream genes, HPLC metabolite profiling, TEAC assay.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential MYB partner in the Del/Ros1 module that robustly activates a broader anthocyanin program than earlier TF combinations used in tomato.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening tomato fruit from mature green to red ripe stages.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct_or_unresolved; inferred direct transcriptional activator as MYB-related TF, but no binding assay in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Transgenic E8::Ros1 construct in combination with E8::Del
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate transcription factor from Antirrhinum majus chosen from prior anthocyanin regulatory studies; cDNA expressed under fruit-specific promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Four independent Del/Ros1 transgenic lines in MicroTom; phenotype introgressed into Money Maker, Ailsa Craig, and VF36.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Cooperates with Del; MYB+bHLH combination is emphasized as necessary for strong activation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and associated modification/transport gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Spectrophotometric anthocyanin assay, HPLC-PDA, ESI-Q-TOF MS/MS, TEAC assay.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Ros1 is a MYB-related transcription factor from snapdragon; expression specificity depends on the tomato fruit E8 promoter used in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization; PROFOOD microarray, ArrayExpress E-MEXP-1830; platform A-MEXP-1417.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Purple pigmentation throughout fruit tissues; no vegetative phenotype reported.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major penalty reported; peel flavonols also increased.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Multiple anthocyanin genes including PAL, F3H, F3'5'H, DFR, ANS, glycosylation genes, putative acyltransferase, GST, and putative anthocyanin transporter.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Together with Del in line N, anthocyanins averaged 2.83 ± 0.46 mg/g fresh weight; hydrophilic antioxidant capacity increased ~3-fold versus wild type.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of broad anthocyanin biosynthetic/modification/transport program when coexpressed with Del.
Regulation_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: MicroTom (also tested in Money Maker, Ailsa Craig, VF36 backgrounds)

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential engineering target for improving anthocyanin sequestration/stability in biofortified fruit, but only putative evidence is provided here.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Induced in Del/Ros1 transgenic ripening fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to vacuolar transport/sequestration of anthocyanins during accumulation in tomato fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identification by suppression subtractive hybridization/microarray as induced transcript; inferred function from annotation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Proposed transporter for anthocyanin vacuolar accumulation, but not functionally validated in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit of Del/Ros1 tomato line N.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: indirect via precursor or transport control
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Suppression subtractive hybridization of Del/Ros1N versus wild-type fruit, followed by validation in expression profiling.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Transgenic line Del/Ros1N versus wild-type control; also supported by microarray across independent lines.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Induced downstream of Del/Ros1 transcription factor module.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Anthocyanin content by spectrophotometry and HPLC in associated transgenic fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Listed in pathway schematic as putative anthocyanin transporter; transporter identity and transport mechanism are not biochemically confirmed here.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization; PROFOOD microarray ArrayExpress E-MEXP-1830, platform A-MEXP-1417.

Title: Enrichment of tomato fruit with health-promoting anthocyanins by expression of select transcription factors
